parentQuestion: To test CN V (trigeminal) motor function, you would ask the patient to ___.

scaffolds.q: The muscles of mastication close the jaw.
scaffolds.explain: Mastication means chewing. The masseter, temporalis, and pterygoid muscles work together to close the jaw with tremendous force and grind food side to side. Feel the weight of this:the power — bite force can exceed 200 pounds per square inch in healthy adults. Incredible strength.

scaffolds.q: CN V has three branches, but only one has motor function.
scaffolds.explain: V1 (ophthalmic) and V2 (maxillary) are purely sensory — they monitor their territories. only V3 (mandibular) carries motor fibers to the jaw muscles. Sensory + motor in one branch. Dual function.
